reviews.review_msg: Overall a good teacher, his lectures are pretty much alright and fun in some occasions; the thing is, he doesn't allow cellphones and also if you don't have a physical copy for the book they are going to read you're not allowed to use a copy from your laptop since electronic devices are not allowed; your only options are printing or buying the book

Matt was one of the best teachers if not the best teachers I have ever had. During my semester with him he was extremely accessible, gave his best to make sure we were well versed in what we were studying and more than anything was able to assist me in performing highly in a subject I was simply just expecting to pass, I would highly recommend him.

actually if you are having different opinion with him, he will argue with you and prove that his are right.
if you get a different opinion with him on essay, you might get a low mark in your essay

Weird gradings
Occasionally rude behaviour
Put little effort in preparation for class

Encourages group discussion in class, doesn't talk for too long. Personally don't think it's a heavy workload (few short essays, reading assigned books/ sections). If you like reading, writing, and exploring ideas, this professor is a good fit. Patient and helpful if you are having difficulty. Clear criteria/ feedback to score from B to A.

An acceptable choice, lectures are fine and give you something to think about afterward. But if you are looking for a high score, do not try to be creative on assignments. If he disagrees or does not understand your creativity, you will score poorly.

Matt pushes his students to be better in their writing, does not direct us to take another path but helps us dig deeper into our own understanding of the topics. Weekly readings are expected and there is an online discussion forum every other week. Overall, he's great, really fun, interesting, likes to make jokes. Just don't sleep/use phone.

Matt is an incredible instructor who teaches course materials that relate to life experiences. He values students' opinions and is more of a group discussion facilitator than a lecturer. He's a bit of a tough grader but knows to reward effort when he sees it so don't try to plagiarize, just do your own work. And absolutely no cellphones in class.
This class is very boring, I regret that I have not chosen another professor. If you like to discuss 2 hrs each week, then go and take his class and good luck with your final exam.

I cannot believe he has that much good comment! I have to say he is tough and I feel I didn't learn anything through this whole semester. Also, he likes to put sensitive political cases in class which make me so uncomfortable. For the essays, you need to write what he thinks, if what you write is different with his expectation, you get a low mark.

pros + supports your opinion + movie(s) + drama(play partially in the class + watch) cons - for some people, taking part in the drama :p i think you can skip it, talking to him - again, for some people, explicit language in literature additional info  NO PHONE during lecture  try to avoid reading from phone, alternate is your laptop ofc
He does not like it when people are on their phones
One of the best instructors, would definitely take another course with him.
Really inspiring and knowledgeable. As long as you do the readingthink muchshould be good.

Matt is a super nice instructor. He made the english literature class fun and interesting. He respects with students regarding to their cultural background. He broadens our vision. He also taught literature differently. He taught us some political science through the reading materials we had.

He's an incredible professor! He's funny and really smart. He respects your opinion and pushes you to better develop your opinions and writing skills. He gives clear criteria for the paper and ton of feedback. And super hot, btw

Okay, I was introduction course in FIC, not for credits. But, it's a mandatory course, so everyone has to pass it in order to transfer to SFU. Matt is great! He is super funny, give a good feedback, always ready to help and give an advice. I am looking forward to take ENGL115 with him.
